conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Our dorm room is getting messy fast. Should we set up some kind of chore schedule for the semester?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, good idea. I hate arguing about whose turn it is to take out the trash.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Exactly. How about we switch jobs every week? One person does dishes, the other vacuums.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That works. But honestly, I'd rather just always do the dishes. I really don't mind them.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, perfect. Then I'll take vacuuming and trash, since you're handling the dishes every time.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sounds fair. Let's write it on the whiteboard so we both remember.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to decorate their dorm room
options.B: Whose turn it is to buy cleaning supplies
options.C: How to divide household chores for the semester
options.D: Whether to move to a different dorm

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：整段对话围绕如何为整个学期制定打扫分工展开，这是核心话题。A选项错误，对话谈的是打扫而不是装饰房间。B选项太窄，谁该买清洁用品根本没有讨论，只是顺带提到倒垃圾的轮值。D选项错误，没有人提到搬到别的宿舍。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely be responsible for?
questions.explanation: 正确答案A：男生明确说他宁愿一直负责洗碗，因为他不介意，所以洗碗归他。B选项与对话相反，吸尘是女生承担的。C选项也与对话相反，倒垃圾同样归女生。D选项过度推断，他们只是说把分工写在白板上，没人提到要买新白板。

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I just joined the fitness center. Can I rent a locker for the whole semester here?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Welcome! Yes, semester lockers are twenty dollars, and you can sign up right at this desk.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Great. Do you also have group classes, like yoga or spin? I'd love to try a few.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: We do. The full class schedule is posted on the app, and most classes are free for members.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll grab a locker now and check the app for a yoga class tonight.

questions.stem: How much does a locker for the whole semester cost?

options.D: The same price as a class

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：工作人员明确说整学期的储物柜是二十美元。B选项错误，免费的是健身课程，不是储物柜。C选项错误，对话从未提到每月十美元这样的价格。D选项错误，对话没有把储物柜价格和课程价格做比较，课程对会员免费。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.B: Sign up for a spin class instead
options.C: Rent a locker and look for a yoga class
options.D: Pay twenty dollars for each class

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：女生说她现在就去租一个储物柜，并在应用上找今晚的瑜伽课，所以她接下来会租柜子并查找瑜伽课。A选项错误，她刚加入健身中心，没有任何取消的意思。B选项错误，她说的是瑜伽课而不是动感单车课。D选项错误，二十美元是储物柜的费用，课程对会员免费。

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: There's a free museum day this Saturday. Do you want to go check out the new exhibits with me?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure! I heard they have a big dinosaur exhibit and a modern art wing. Which one sounds better to you?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honestly, the dinosaur one. I've seen the art wing twice already, and the fossils are supposed to be amazing.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Works for me. Should we get there early? Those popular exhibits get really crowded by noon.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Definitely. Let's meet at nine so we can see the fossils before the crowds show up.

questions.stem: Which exhibit does the woman want to see?
questions.explanation: 正确答案B：女生说她想看恐龙化石那个展，因为艺术馆她已经去过两次了。A选项与对话相反，艺术馆是她已经看腻的部分。C选项错误，对话从未提到摄影展。D选项错误，她明确选了恐龙展，并没有说一天看完两个展。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about why they plan to arrive early?

options.B: The fossils are only shown in the morning
options.C: They want to see the art wing first
options.D: They want to avoid the midday crowds

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：男生说热门展到中午会很拥挤，女生同意九点见面，好在人群涌入前看化石，所以早到是为了避开中午的人潮。A选项错误，对话只说中午变挤，没说博物馆中午关门。B选项过度推断，没人说化石只在早上展出。C选项与对话相反，女生已经看过艺术馆，他们要先看的是化石。
